text:
REGIME — Risk-on, tech and discretionary led, energy collapsed, metals surged.

INDICES — VIX 18.4, falling. QQQ +1.82%, IWM +1.38%, SPY +1.22%, DIA +0.70%. Small caps outperformed large caps. Silver surged +5.51%, the standout cross-asset move of the session.

SECTORS — XLY +2.21%, XLK +1.60%, XLC +1.52%, XLRE +0.95%, XLV +0.58%, XLI +0.36%, XLF +0.23%, XLU +0.17%, XLP -0.10%, XLB -0.34%, XLE -2.03%. Top industries: WGMI +6.43%, SLV +5.51%. Bottom industries: FCG -3.30%, USO -3.60%.

THE 7s — Mag 7 +3.01%, Cloud 7 +2.42%, New Staples 7 +2.42%, Fab 7 +2.19%, Old Metals 7 +1.38%, Semi 7 +1.35%, Medtech 7 +0.98%, Buildout 7 +0.96%, New Metals 7 +0.66%, Power 7 +0.64%, Freight 7 +0.62%, Bank 7 +0.35%, Health 7 +0.34%, Industrial 7 +0.01%, Defense 7 -0.01%, Housing 7 -0.07%, Old Staples 7 -0.19%, Toll Road 7 -0.35%, Insurance 7 -0.64%, Software 7 -0.66%, Energy 7 -2.51%. Strongest in Mag 7: ORCL +4.74%. Weakest in Energy 7 tied to USO -3.60%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29; CME shows 100% hold, 0% cut, 0% hike. Fed stance: 6 hawks (Cook, Hammack, Kashkari, Logan, Barkin, Goolsbee), 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD liquidity composite 38/100 (Normal); most-stressed component is Reserve Scarcity at 80th percentile. ON-RRP at $0.3B, TED spread 0.09%, both benign.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 60.6, Greed. Top movers: WFC -5.70% (Financials, vol 2.1x), WGMI +6.43% (Industries), SLV +5.51% (Cross-Asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLK +6.73%, worst XLP -3.59%; best basket Semi 7 +22.21%, worst Software 7 -12.64%. WFC dropped -5.70% despite an EPS beat as mixed Q1 results and weak net interest income guidance spooked traders.

WATCH — WFC -5.70% (vol 2.1x): earnings reaction selling into prior strength warrants a level check at $85. RSPM -0.40% (vol 5.4x): unusual volume with minimal price move signals positioning activity worth tracking. ORCL +4.74% (vol 2.5x): momentum break higher with heavy volume — watch for follow-through or fade.
